[cron:feee69f8-6016-4f8f-a09c-08b39d9a9191 github-backup] Run these commands: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push). Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.

content.thinking: This is a cron job that runs git backup commands. Let me execute the commands to check if there are any changes to commit.

The git backup succeeded - it committed and pushed changes. Since there was something to commit (not "nothing to commit"), but it succeeded, I should NOT send a message to Nemesis. The instructions say to only report if there was an error.

So I should reply NO_REPLY since nothing went wrong.
